name: Databricks coding-agent benchmark
task_surface: Recent human-written pull requests from a multi-million-line internal codebase
slide_lead: Databricks evaluated recent internal pull requests: Pi used about one-third as much context, and equal-quality runs sometimes differed by more than 2 times in cost.
method_summary: Databricks replayed recent human-written pull requests from its multi-million-line internal codebase. Engineers reviewed whether each agent's changes passed repository tests and matched the intended change.
conclusion: Pi used about one-third as much context per turn. Some equal-quality matched runs differed by more than 2 times in cost.
limitation: The source does not publish task count, matched rows, run-level data or uncertainty.

name: Warden benchmark
task_surface: Known-vulnerability recognition in files tied to historical Sentry vulnerabilities
slide_lead: Sentry tested 86 known vulnerabilities: Pi found a similar or larger share while costing about one-quarter to one-fifth as much as Claude SDK.
method_summary: Sentry gave agents files connected to 86 historical vulnerabilities and measured how many known findings they recovered. The published table compares matched Claude models running in Pi and Claude SDK.
conclusion: Pi cost less and found a similar or larger share of the known corpus for the matched Claude models.
limitation: The comparator is Claude SDK, not the Claude Code command-line product. The benchmark does not measure whole-repository discovery.

name: scBench-Long
slide_lead: scBench-Long repeated 21 biological workflows: the apparent harness differences are smaller than the reported uncertainty.
method_summary: LatchBio ran 21 long biological analysis workflows 3 times for each model and harness combination. Deterministic biological graders checked whether the analyses reached the required outputs.
conclusion: Claude Code led Pi on Opus 4.8 while Codex and Pi tied on GPT-5.5. The reported intervals overlap widely.
limitation: The public summary does not expose cost. The confidence intervals are wide.

name: OpenBench M4.5
task_surface: Three repository coding tasks with executable checkers
slide_lead: OpenBench repeated 3 coding tasks: every harness passed, so the useful difference was efficiency rather than correctness.
method_summary: OpenBench ran 3 repository coding tasks 3 times with GPT-5.5 at medium effort. Executable checkers awarded partial credit, while the study also recorded wall time and token use.
conclusion: All clean harnesses passed every task. Pi and Cursor used less time and fewer tokens.
limitation: The tasks were too easy to separate frontier harnesses on correctness.

name: DrugDiscoveryBench
grader: Expert-authored rubrics scored by an LLM judge
data_access: Tasks are public; rubrics and reference answers require access approval
slide_lead: DrugDiscoveryBench repeated 82 expert workflows: mini-SWE-agent led the matched GPT-5.5 comparison, while Codex placed in the middle.
method_summary: Scale AI ran 82 expert-curated drug-discovery and life-sciences workflows 3 times. An LLM judge scored each result against expert-authored rubrics and reference answers.
conclusion: GPT-5.5 performed best in mini-SWE-agent. Codex was neither the best nor worst harness for that model.
limitation: The paper plots cost but does not publish a table of exact cost points. The outcome depends on an LLM judge.

name: Coding Agent Index
task_surface: 321 tasks across DeepSWE, Terminal-Bench v2 and SWE-Atlas-QnA
grader: Program checks, test suites and rubric scoring
slide_lead: Artificial Analysis combined 321 coding tasks: OpenCode scored higher with Opus 4.7, while Claude Code was cheaper and faster.
method_summary: Artificial Analysis combines 321 tasks from DeepSWE, Terminal-Bench v2 and SWE-Atlas-QnA. It repeats each configuration and reports a composite score alongside cost and runtime.
conclusion: OpenCode led the captured Opus 4.7 medium comparison. Claude Code was cheaper and faster.
limitation: The index averages 3 different benchmark families. The live results and harness versions can change.

name: Agent Security League
task_surface: Real vulnerability-fixing tasks with hidden functional and security tests
slide_lead: Endor Labs tested 200 vulnerability repairs: Cursor led for Opus, but Claude Code led for Sonnet 5.
method_summary: Endor Labs asked each configuration to repair 200 real vulnerabilities. Hidden tests separately measured whether the software still worked and whether the vulnerability was fixed.
conclusion: Cursor led Claude Code for Opus 4.7 and 4.8. Claude Code led Cursor for Sonnet 5.
limitation: There are no repeated trials or task-level costs. Timeouts explain part of the reversal.

name: Vulnerability-Agent-Bench
task_surface: Real vulnerability repairs in isolated containers
slide_lead: XOR tested 136 vulnerability repairs: Claude Code and Codex beat OpenCode on all 3 published matched-model pairs.
method_summary: XOR ran agents in isolated containers on real vulnerability-repair tasks. Its public explorer reports pass rate and cost for matched model and harness configurations.
conclusion: Claude Code and Codex led the corresponding OpenCode configurations on the published matched pairs.
limitation: The site alternates between 128 and 136 samples. Some reported cost differences need more explanation.

name: WildClawBench
task_surface: Bilingual, multimodal and long-horizon agent tasks
grader: Deterministic checks, environment checks and model judges
data_access: Code, tasks, images and evaluation details
slide_lead: WildClawBench tested 60 long-horizon tasks: changing the harness produced double-digit score shifts and changed the winner by model.
method_summary: InternLM evaluated 60 bilingual, multimodal and long-horizon tasks in harness-specific container images. Deterministic checks, environment checks and model judges contributed to the overall score.
conclusion: The best harness changed with the model. The source reports double-digit score changes from switching harness.
limitation: Each harness image has different tools and prompt conventions. Repeat counts are not clear in the headline table.

name: HarnessBench
task_surface: Recent bug-fix issues from 9 open-source repositories
slide_lead: HarnessBench tested 27 recent bug fixes: several configurations finished within one task of the leader, but runtime differed more clearly.
method_summary: HarnessBench selected 27 recent bug-fix issues from 9 open-source repositories. Hidden tests checked the core fix and regressions, with one run for each configuration.
conclusion: Several conditions finished within one task of the observed leader. Runtime differences were clearer than pass-rate differences.
limitation: The author states that the 27-task pass-rate differences are not statistically significant.

name: CORE-Bench Hard
slide_lead: CORE-Bench Hard tested 45 scientific reproductions: Claude Code more than doubled verified success while costing less than CORE-Agent.
method_summary: Princeton HAL asked agents to reproduce 45 research artefacts. The leaderboard verifies whether each system recreated the required scientific result and reports total cost.
conclusion: Claude Code more than doubled the verified success of CORE-Agent for Claude Opus 4.5 while costing less.
limitation: There is one run per configuration. The systems differ across retrieval, tools and context policy.

name: A-CODE-CLI
grader: Backend checks and Playwright user-interface checks
data_access: Public report and one example task
slide_lead: A-CODE-CLI repeated 10 web scenarios: OpenCode had a 2.4-point backend lead and cost 44% less than Claude Code.
method_summary: AIMultiple ran 10 full-stack web development scenarios 3 times with Claude Sonnet 4.6. Backend checks and Playwright tests measured correctness and user-interface behaviour.
conclusion: OpenCode had a small backend lead over Claude Code and cost less in the matched Sonnet 4.6 comparison.
limitation: The benchmark has only 10 scenarios. Only one example task is public.

name: Harness Tax
task_surface: Two trivial prompts routed through a gateway
slide_lead: Portkey measured 2 trivial requests: fixed input overhead ranged from about 2,600 tokens for Pi to 27,000 for Claude Code.
method_summary: Portkey routed 2 trivial prompts through its gateway and recorded the input payload sent by each harness. The exercise isolates fixed prompt overhead rather than coding performance.
conclusion: The fixed input payload ranged from about 2,600 tokens for Pi to 27,000 for Claude Code.
limitation: This is an overhead diagnostic. It does not measure coding quality or full-session economics.
